data.title: Sphere 3D Corp expected to post a loss of 27 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Sphere 3D Corp is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on March 11 (estimated) for the period ending December 31 2024

  • The Toronto Ontario-based company is expected to report a 63.6% decrease in revenue to $2.8 million from $7.69 million a year ago, according to the estimate from one analyst, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Sphere 3D Corp is for a loss of 27 cents per share.

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "buy".

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Sphere 3D Corp is $3.00​, above​ its last closing price of $0.57. ​​​

data.title: Metals Exploration Pays Off Loan via Share Issue
data.description:
Metals Exploration plc amended the terms of its agreement with Drachs Investments No3 to repay the unsecured bridging term loan facility through equity, according to a Friday release.

To settle an outstanding loan of 5.6 million pounds sterling, the gold producer issued 94,127,854 shares at 0.06 pound per share. Drachs now holds 16.12% of the company's total voting rights.

The equity repayment will help Metals Exploration preserve cash for the exploration and development of its assets in the Philippines and Nicaragua.

data.title: German wind supply set to fall on Monday
data.description:
German and French prompt power prices for Monday were untraded on Friday as the market weighed an expected drop in wind power throughout the region against a general drop in demand.

Both the German and French Monday baseload power prices were untraded by 1050 GMT, LSEG data showed. (TRDEBD3), (TRFRBD3)

German wind power output was expected to fall by 6.4 gigawatts (GW) to 2.7 GW on Monday while that from France was projected to drop by 7.3 GW to 1.8 GW, according to LSEG data.

German residual load is increasing week-on-week on Monday on the back of a significant drop in renewable supplies, LSEG analyst Riccardo Parviero said.

French nuclear availability fell two percentage points to 77% of total capacity as the Paluel 3 reactor went offline with an unplanned outage.

Operator EDF said the Paluel 3 reactor was taken offline to carry out tests on a pump supplying the primary circuit of the unit.

Power consumption in Germany is forecast to slide 870 megawatts (MW) to 58.7 GW, while demand in France is expected to fall 2.5 GW to 51.1 GW on Monday, LSEG data showed.

German year-ahead power (TRDEBYZ6) gained 2.6% to 82.50 euros per megawatt hour (MWh), while the French 2025 baseload contract (TRFRBYZ6) was untraded with a bid price of 62.10 euros/MWh.

In the European carbon market (CFI2Zc1), the benchmark contract rose 2% to 68.69 euros a metric ton.

data.title: Futures inch up with payrolls data, Powell's remarks on tap
data.description:
Key points:
  • Futures up: Dow 0.15%, S&P 500 0.31%, Nasdaq 0.48%

U.S. stock index futures edged up on Friday, ahead of a key jobs report and comments from Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell, as investors keenly awaited clarity on the health of the world's largest economy amid turbulence triggered by tariff uncertainty.

At 05:18 a.m. ET, Dow E-minis were up 66 points, or 0.15%, S&P 500 E-minis were up 18.25 points, or 0.31% and Nasdaq 100 E-minis were up 97 points, or 0.48%.

The Labor Department's nonfarm payrolls report is likely to show the economy added 160,000 jobs in February, higher than the 143,000 reported in the month before.

February unemployment rate is likely to remain unchanged from January at 4%, and could offer comfort to investors navigating signs of deteriorating consumer and business confidence. However, recent initiatives to cut government spending could threaten the job market resilience in the months ahead.

Among top movers, Broadcom rose 12.6% in premarket trading after the chipmaker assuaged investor worries about artificial intelligence infrastructure demand with a strong second-quarter forecast - a day after peer Marvell's in-line forecast disappointed investors.

Marvell rose 1.5% after Thursday's 20% slide, while Nvidia and Micron added 1.3% and 1.5%, respectively.

Equities witnessed their most volatile week this year, with Wall Street's fear gauge trading near levels not seen since mid-December, as traders tried to assess President Donald Trump's fluctuating trade policy. The index was muted on Friday.

In the previous session, the Nasdaq confirmed a 10% drop from its December all-time high, while the benchmark S&P 500 appeared to have reversed most of its gains since Trump's election victory.

The indexes, along with the blue-chip Dow are on track for their biggest weekly drop since September.

Analysts say the concerns are that Trump's tariffs would push the world's largest economy into a period of high inflation and slowing growth.

Trump on Wednesday offered a four-week reprieve on tariffs he imposed on imports from Canada and Mexico that fall under a free-trade pact, but the U.S. is still in a trade war with China. Additionally, reciprocal trade barriers and other duties are expected to take effect in the following weeks.

Traders now see the Fed lowering interest rates by at least 75 basis points this year, according to data compiled by LSEG - more than what they were pricing-in earlier in the year.

Comments from Chair Powell at 12:30 p.m. ET could offer more clarity on the central bank's policy. His colleagues, including New York Fed President John Williams, Governors Michelle Bowman and Adriana Kugler, are also slated to speak later in the day.

Gap beat fourth-quarter sales and profit estimates, sending shares of the apparel company up 17.8%.

Costco dipped 1.7% after the retailer missed Street estimates on quarterly earnings as merchandise costs increased.

Walgreens jumped 5.6% after the pharmacy chain agreed to be taken private by Sycamore Partners for $10 billion.

data.title: European bank rally 'faster and stronger' than predicted - UBS
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 down 0.7%
  • Trump suspends some Canada, Mexico tariffs
  • Wall St futures higher

EUROPEAN BANK RALLY 'FASTER AND STRONGER' THAN PREDICTED - UBS

The rally in European banks so far in 2025 has been faster and stronger than UBS analysts expected, with the space up more that double versus the wider market year-to-date.

A basket of European bank stocks (.SX7P) is up 25.2% so far this year according to LSEG Workspace while the benchmark STOXX 600 is up 8.6%.

In fact, UBS points out that a quarter of the STOXX 600's top 20 performers this year so far are banks; namely Societe Generale , Sabadell , Commerzbank , Santander and Banca Popolare di Sondrio .

So what is behind the blistering performance?

UBS highlights five aspects.

The first two are fairly straightforward; the 10% equity rally itself and a return of the Eurozone macro surprise index to positive territory.

The third is a stellar fourth quarter results season in which all of the banks that UBS tracks beat consensus revenue forecasts.

Earnings revision weakness outside of banks is also a contributing element and the fourth in their list.

Finally, in fifth place is the partial unwind of the fourth quarter 2024 consensus trade of buying U.S. banks for 10% forecast average EPS growth, higher terminal rates, stronger forecast nominal GDP, deregulation and a stronger dollar.

(Lucy Raitano)

*****

data.title: Chile's consumer prices up 0.4% m/m in February
data.description:
Consumer prices in Chile rose 0.4% in February from the previous month, data from statistics agency INE showed on Friday, in line with market expectations in a Reuters poll of economists.

Annual inflation in the world's largest copper producer hit 4.7%, INE added, slowing from the 4.9% registered the previous month but still above the central bank's 2% to 4% target range.

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Broadcom, Nvidia, HPE, Gap, Walgreens, Costco, Intuitive Machines, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stock futures were rising Friday ahead of the release of the monthly U.S. jobs report following a Wall Street selloff that had investors rattled by frequent shifts in trade policy from the Trump administration. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite finished Thursday in correction territory.

These stocks were poised to make moves Friday:

Broadcom rose 13% after the semiconductor and software posted better-than-expected fiscal first-quarter earnings and issued strong guidance. Broadcom reported adjusted earnings in the first quarter of $1.60 a share, higher than analysts' expectations of $1.51. Revenue of $14.92 topped consensus of $14.62 billion. Artificial-intelligence revenue in the quarter was $4.1 billion, up 77% from a year earlier. For the current second quarter, Broadcom anticipates revenue of $14.9 billion compared with expectations of $14.71 billion. CEO Hock Tan said Broadcom's largest technology customers continue to "invest aggressively" in their next generation AI models.

Hewlett Packard Enterprise sank 20% after fiscal first-quarter earnings and second-quarter guidance missed Wall Street expectations and the server and cloud-software company said it would begin a cost-cutting program that will reduce its overall headcount by about 5%, or about 2,500 jobs. HPE said it expects second-quarter adjusted earnings of 28 cents to 34 cents a share on revenue of $7.2 billion to $7.6 billion. Analysts expect adjusted earnings of 50 cents on revenue of $7.92 billion. As for the tariffs situation, CEO Antonio Neri told Barron's that it was a "very fluid, complicated situation, with a lot of moving parts and twists." The CEO said HPE does a lot of manufacturing in Mexico.

Marvell Technology was up 1.5% in premarket trading after the chip maker sank nearly 20% on Thursday, the stock's largest percentage decrease since Sept. 27, 2001, when it fell 32%, according to Dow Jones Market Data. On Wednesday, Marvell issued fiscal first-quarter guidance that was mostly in line with expectations, disappointing investors who had thought the company might offer even more positive forecasts.

Gap was jumping 18% after the apparel retailer topped fourth-quarter earnings expectations and delivered stronger-than-expected profit guidance for the current fiscal year. Gap sees total company sales in the fiscal year rising between 1% to 2% versus Wall Street expectations of a 1.7% increase to $15.7 billion. The retailer projects an increase in operating income of between 8% and 10% from $1.1 billion in 2024.

Walgreens Boots Alliance will be taken private by Sycamore Partners in a $23.7 billion deal. Sycamore will pay $11.45 a share in cash, representing an equity value of around $10 billion. Shareholders also will receive up to $3 in cash per share representing the future sale of the company's debt and equity interests in VillageMD. The companies expect the deal to close in the fourth quarter. Walgreens rose 5.6% to $11.19 in premarket trading.

Fiscal second-quarter earnings at Costco Wholesale, the warehouse retailer, missed analysts' estimates, sending shares down 1.3% in premarket trading. Costco reported earnings of $4.02 a share, below estimates of $4.09, as revenue of $63.7 billion was slightly better than projections of $63.1 billion. Same-store sales rose 6.8% and beat estimates that called for a 6.4% increase.

Intuitive Machines was tumbling 35% after difficulties landing the company's Athena spaceship near the moon's south pole on Thursday. The craft touched down around noon Eastern time, but NASA's broadcast of the landing finished without clear information on the status of the craft.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Bimini Capital Management Announces Fourth Quarter 2024 Results
data.description:
Bimini Capital Management, Inc., a company primarily investing in residential mortgage-related securities, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024. The company reported a net loss, reflecting the challenges faced in the fixed income market during the period.

Financial Highlights

For the three-month period ended December 31, 2024, Bimini Capital Management reported a net loss of $1.5 million, translating to a loss of $0.15 per common share. The book value per share stood at $0.68. The company’s advisory service revenue saw a slight increase, reaching $3.4 million compared to $3.3 million in the previous quarter. The investment portfolio generated net interest income of $0.3 million, with dividends on Orchid stock contributing $0.2 million.

Business and Operational Highlights

Orchid Island Capital, managed by Bimini’s subsidiary Bimini Advisors, reported a net income of $5.6 million for the fourth quarter of 2024. This resulted in a slight increase in Bimini's advisory service revenues. The MBS portfolio saw a net increase of $4.0 million during the quarter, contributing to a total increase of $29.5 million for the year. The company also reported positive cash flows from operations for both the quarter and the full year.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

During the fourth quarter, Bimini Capital Management continued to grow its MBS portfolio, leveraging positive cash flows. The company also updated its projected utilization of deferred tax assets, resulting in a tax provision of $2.1 million. This adjustment contributed to the net loss reported for the quarter.

Management's Perspective

Robert E. Cauley, Chairman and CEO, commented on the fourth quarter results, noting the shift in the fixed income market outlook early in the quarter. Despite the economic outlook shifting, the Fed lowered the Fed Funds rate twice during the quarter, leading to a dis-inversion of the yield curve. Cauley highlighted the strong economic outlook and the positive market conditions for both Orchid Island and Royal Palm's investment portfolios.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, Bimini Capital Management remains cautious about the economic outlook as the first quarter of 2025 progresses. The company acknowledges the uncertainty in the market and its potential impact on interest rate levels, Federal Reserve monetary policy, and the MBS market. However, current market conditions have been favorable for the company’s investment portfolios.

SEC Filing: BIMINI CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC. [ BMNM ] - 8-K - Mar. 06, 2025

data.title: Euro Set for Best Week in 16 Years
data.description:
The euro surged above $1.085, reaching its highest level since early November and heading for a 4.6% weekly gain, its strongest performance since March 2009, driven by Germany’s fiscal reforms and a more cautious stance from the European Central Bank on future rate cuts.

Germany’s major political parties announced plans to reform the country’s debt brake, allowing for increased defense spending and a €500 billion infrastructure initiative aimed at boosting economic growth.

Additionally, European leaders agreed on Thursday to a significant increase in defense spending to strengthen Europe’s defense industry and enhance military capabilities.

On the policy front, the ECB delivered a widely expected 25bps rate cut and acknowledged that monetary policy is becoming less restrictive, signaling a possible pause in further reductions.

Traders anticipate one or two additional 25bps cuts later this year.

data.title: Middle East Crude-Benchmarks gain; Saudi lowers OSP to Asia
data.description:
Middle East crude benchmark spot premiums of Oman, Dubai and Murban climbed on Friday and posted small gains for this week.

Saudi Arabia, the world's top oil exporter, on Friday lowered crude oil prices for Asian buyers in April for the first time in three months, in line with market expectations and after OPEC+ agreed to gradually increase supply in the same month.

State oil company Saudi Aramco lowered the April official selling price (OSP) for flagship Arab Light crude by 40 cents to $3.50 a barrel above the average of Oman and Dubai prices, a pricing document from the producer showed.

The company also lowered April prices for other grades it sells to Asia.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps rose 20 cents to $1.37 a barrel.

PetroChina will deliver two May-loading Upper Zakum crude cargoes to Vitol following the deals.

Meanwhile, Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) bought two partials using S&P Global Platts' pricing process for Middle East benchmark Dubai crude oil, traders said on Friday.

NEWS

China's crude oil imports fell 5% in the first two months of 2025 versus year-ago levels, data showed on Friday, as tougher U.S. sanctions on ships carrying Russian and Iranian oil and a Chinese port ban slowed flows into the world's top importer.

Shell has begun production at Phase 4 of its Sabah deepwater oil project in Malaysian and Bruneian waters, it said on Friday.

Exports of Kazakhstan's CPC Blend to Asia are expected to stay high in March and April after hitting their highest in well over a year, according to traders and shipping data, as Chinese and South Korean buyers replaced more expensive Middle Eastern oil.

Norwegian oil company DNO has agreed to buy rival Sval Energi from private equity group HitecVision in a deal valued at $1.6 billion, it said on Friday, marking the latest case of consolidation among North Sea oil and gas producers.

Indian state refiner Bharat Petroleum Corp plans to float a 4-month tender next week to buy U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude, a person familiar with the matter said on Friday, as it eyes cheaper oil from the world's top producer.

data.title: Hunting plc Buys Organic Oil Recovery Portfolio for $17.5 Million
data.description:
Hunting plc said Friday it acquired an organic oil recovery technology from its founding shareholders for $17.5 million.

The British precision engineering group's acquisition includes 25 patents, distribution rights and a US-based laboratory, allowing the company to advance the technology's international commercialization. The deal includes a 15% royalty payment to sellers for revenue earned within 15 years after the deal's completion.

Hunting added that free trials of the technology with blue-chip exploration and production companies in North America, Europe, the Middle East, and Asia Pacific are ongoing.

data.title: China Foreign Exchange Reserves Rise in February
data.description:
China’s foreign exchange reserves rose to $3.22 trillion in February 2025 from $3.209 trillion in January and roughly in line with forecasts.

The yuan appreciated 0.5% against the dollar in February, while the dollar weakened 0.9% against a basket of other major currencies.

Meanwhile, gold reserves went up for a fourth consecutive month to 73.61 million fine troy ounces, above 73.45 million in the previous month.

Their value jumped to $208.64 billion from $206.53 billion, in line with a surge in gold prices.
